chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

questions.question: Which organization became the main platform for Indian nationalists in the late nineteenth century?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress became the primary platform for political discussions and nationalist activities.

questions.question: When was the Indian National Congress founded?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885.

questions.question: Which early nationalist leader strongly criticized British economic policies in India?
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji criticized British economic exploitation through the Drain of Wealth theory.

questions.question: What was the main method used by moderate leaders in the early national movement?
questions.explanation: Moderate leaders believed in peaceful constitutional methods such as petitions and discussions.

questions.question: Which event triggered the Swadeshi Movement?
questions.explanation: The Swadeshi Movement began as a protest against the Partition of Bengal in 1905.

questions.question: What did the Swadeshi Movement encourage people to do?
questions.explanation: The movement encouraged people to boycott British goods and promote Indian products.

questions.question: Who led the Salt March in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Salt March to protest the British salt tax.

questions.question: The Salt March was part of which movement?
questions.explanation: The Salt March was a major event during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.question: What was the main idea behind Gandhi’s concept of Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Satyagraha means the force of truth and nonviolent resistance.

questions.question: Which movement was launched by Gandhi in 1920?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in 1920.

questions.question: Why did Gandhi withdraw the Non-Cooperation Movement?

options.B: Violence during the Chauri Chaura incident

questions.explanation: Gandhi withdrew the movement after the Chauri Chaura incident turned violent.

questions.question: Which year did the Civil Disobedience Movement begin?
questions.explanation: The Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930 with the Salt March.

questions.question: Which section of society actively participated in the national movement?
questions.explanation: Farmers, workers, students, and women participated in the national movement.

questions.question: When was the Quit India Movement launched?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched in 1942.

questions.question: Which leader gave the slogan 'Do or Die'?
questions.explanation: Gandhi gave the slogan 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement.

questions.question: Which global conflict influenced the political situation in India during the 1940s?
questions.explanation: World War II weakened British power and strengthened the demand for independence.

questions.question: In which year did India gain independence?
questions.explanation: India gained independence on 15 August 1947.

questions.question: Which new country was created along with India in 1947?
questions.explanation: Pakistan was created during the partition of India in 1947.

questions.question: What was one major consequence of the partition of India?
questions.explanation: Millions of people migrated between India and Pakistan after partition.

questions.question: Which principle helped maintain unity in the Indian national movement?
questions.explanation: Unity among people from different regions and communities strengthened the national movement.

questions.question: Which method was widely used by Gandhi to oppose British rule?
questions.explanation: Gandhi promoted nonviolent protest and peaceful resistance.

questions.question: Which slogan was associated with the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: All these slogans were used during the Indian freedom struggle.

questions.question: Which feature made the Indian national movement unique?
questions.explanation: The movement is notable for its nonviolent mass participation.

questions.question: Which idea inspired many independence movements worldwide?
questions.explanation: Gandhi’s idea of nonviolent struggle inspired movements across the world.

questions.question: What is the overall significance of the Indian national movement?
questions.explanation: The Indian national movement led to independence through a democratic struggle.
